title: COMPLAINING AT A RESTAURANT
conversation: At Carmen’s Bistro, all is not going well. John complains to Kevin, the waiter, about his overdone steak. Tanya complains that her fish is too rare and that the music is too loud. Kevin: How are we doing? John: Not very well. I ordered my steak medium rare, and it’s burnt to a crisp. Kevin: I’m sorry about that. I can ask our chef to prepare another one. John: Okay, please do that. Kevin: You bet. And how’s your fish, ma’am? Tanya: It’s very rare. Kevin: Would you like me to have the chef put it back in the oven? Tanya: No, I’ve lost my appetite for fish. Kevin: I’d be happy to bring you something else. Tanya: Okay, let me try the stuffed chicken breast. (Ten minutes after the waiter has brought the new meals.) Kevin: How are we doing over here? Tanya: I was wondering if you could turn down the music. Loud music in restaurants is a pet peeve of mine. Kevin: Sure, I’ll take care of that. Can I get you another round of drinks? John: No, we’re all set for now. (The waiter returns after fifteen minutes.) Kevin: Are you still working on your meals? Tanya: I’ll need a doggy bag. John: Would you mind wrapping this up, too? Kevin: I’ll bring you some containers and you can box up your leftovers. Can I interest you in dessert? Tanya: I’m stuffed. John: Could you please bring the check?

idioms.meaning_en: meat cooked so that it is still red and warm in the middle
idioms.example: I'd like my steak medium rare, please.
idioms.context: I ordered my steak medium rare, and it’s burnt to a crisp.

idioms.example: The toast was left in the toaster too long and was burnt to a crisp.
idioms.context: I ordered my steak medium rare, and it’s burnt to a crisp.

idioms.meaning_en: certainly; you're welcome; used to express agreement or to say yes
idioms.meaning_vi: chắc chắn rồi, không có gì
idioms.example: "Can you help me with this?" "You bet!"
idioms.context: Kevin: You bet. And how’s your fish, ma’am?

idioms.meaning_en: to no longer feel hungry, especially because of something unpleasant
idioms.example: After seeing a fly in the soup, I completely lost my appetite.
idioms.context: No, I’ve lost my appetite for fish.

idioms.meaning_en: to reduce the volume, heat, or intensity of something
idioms.meaning_vi: vặn nhỏ lại, giảm âm lượng
idioms.example: Could you please turn down the radio? It's too loud.
idioms.context: I was wondering if you could turn down the music.

idioms.meaning_en: something that a particular person finds especially annoying
idioms.meaning_vi: điều gây khó chịu hoặc phiền toái cá nhân
idioms.example: One of my pet peeves is people who chew with their mouths open.
idioms.context: Loud music in restaurants is a pet peeve of mine.

idioms.meaning_en: a set of drinks served to everyone in a group at one time
idioms.meaning_vi: một lượt đồ uống cho cả bàn
idioms.example: The host ordered a round of drinks for everyone at the table.
idioms.context: Can I get you another round of drinks?

idioms.meaning_en: to be finished, ready, or having everything that is needed
idioms.meaning_vi: xong rồi, chuẩn bị xong, đủ rồi
idioms.example: I don't need any more help, I'm all set.
idioms.context: No, we’re all set for now.

idioms.meaning_en: in the process of eating or doing something
idioms.meaning_vi: vẫn đang dùng, vẫn đang ăn
idioms.example: Are you still working on that salad, or can I take the plate?
idioms.context: Are you still working on your meals?

idioms.meaning_en: a container or bag used to take home leftover food from a restaurant
idioms.meaning_vi: túi/hộp đựng thức ăn thừa mang về
idioms.example: The portions were huge, so I asked for a doggy bag.

idioms.meaning_en: to pack food to take away; to finish something
idioms.example: Since we can't finish the pizza, could you wrap it up for us?
idioms.context: Would you mind wrapping this up, too?

idioms.meaning_en: to put something (usually food) into a box or container
idioms.example: I'll box up the rest of the cake so you can take it home.
idioms.context: I’ll bring you some containers and you can box up your leftovers.

idioms.meaning_en: food that remains uneaten at the end of a meal
idioms.example: We had pizza leftovers for breakfast the next morning.
idioms.context: I’ll bring you some containers and you can box up your leftovers.

idioms.meaning_en: a polite way to offer something to someone
idioms.meaning_vi: Tôi có thể mời bạn dùng... không?
idioms.example: Can I interest you in a cup of coffee after dinner?
idioms.context: Can I interest you in dessert?

idioms.meaning_en: feeling very full after eating a lot of food
idioms.example: I've eaten so much turkey that I'm absolutely stuffed.
